qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Synthetic RMI context propagation endpoint deserializes attacker-controlled objects
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same synthetic RMI/JMX context endpoint and the same unfiltered ObjectInput.readObject() deserialization path described by the advisory.

selected_finding.title: Synthetic RMI context propagation endpoint deserializes attacker-controlled objects

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's vulnerable RMI context propagation endpoint and its copied PoC demonstrated attacker-controlled gadget execution inside the instrumented JVM.
evidence: The advisory and the finding both describe a custom RMI/JMX context endpoint introduced by the OpenTelemetry Java agent that deserializes untrusted network data without serialization filters.
The finding traces the vulnerable path through RmiServerContextInstrumentation -> ContextDispatcher -> ContextPayload.read() -> ObjectInput.readObject(), which is the advisory-described root cause.

The copied PoC run at /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_6cf6a998/.judge/poc_runs/rmi_context_unsafe_deserialization/run.log completed successfully and recorded a unique EVOHUNT_RMI_CONTEXT_POC marker emitted from ExploitGadget.readObject() after the forged CONTEXT_CALL_ID request while legitimate health checks still succeeded before and after exploitation.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC launched an instrumented RMI server, sent a forged CONTEXT_CALL_ID request, and the run log shows Reproduction succeeded with a unique EVOHUNT_RMI_CONTEXT_POC marker emitted from ExploitGadget.readObject() and reflected in the proof-file check while baseline and post-exploit health checks both remained HEALTH_OK.
